examples.title: Non-Monotonic Reasoning
examples.description: Demonstrates how to retract conclusions when new evidence emerges
examples.code:
ALGORITHM NonMonotonicReasoning
INPUT: Initial Knowledge, New Evidence
OUTPUT: Revised Conclusion

BEGIN
 1. Draw conclusions from Initial Knowledge
 2. Evaluate New Evidence
 3. Retract conclusions if New Evidence invalidates them
 4. Draw new conclusions based on updated knowledge
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates the process of non-monotonic reasoning, where new evidence can lead to the retraction of previous conclusions.

examples.title: Fuzzy Logic
examples.description: Represents vague concepts using membership functions
examples.explanation: This Python code defines a function to calculate the degree of membership in fuzzy sets for vague concepts like temperature.

examples.title: Default Reasoning
examples.description: Makes assumptions about typical cases when specific information is lacking
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates default reasoning, where we assume birds typically fly unless we have evidence to the contrary (e.g., penguins).
